rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D ET AL VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday September 4, 2019 line 1 - DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND's Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. At the hearing, the parties should be prepared to address the following: (1) Why the requested discovery is necessary to enable Plaintiffs to prepare a complaint (as opposed to discovery once a complaint is properly settled). As indicated in the Order of 4/19/2019, "the discovery must be directly related to ascertaining evidence to support the allegations in the complaint, namely State Fund's conduct regarding Dr. Cabayan." (2) Why an order on this motion will impact the proceedings on the order to show cause (see 8/9/2019 O'Connor Supplemental Decl.) (3) Whether there are email or other sources such as personal or shared drives of key custodians that could identify responsive documents without necessarily relying on the CARE claims systems, and if so, whether review from those sources can be prioritized. (4) Why State Fund produced documents such as WCAB or payment records that were excluded in the request. (5) Why the Court should not order a privilege log. (6) Why the documents sought by RFP 3, 5 are relevant. (7) Whether it is appropriate to limit the request in RFP 3, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 from "all documents relating to" to "documents sufficient to show" communications with third parties. (8) Sanctions to be addressed after resolving merits of motion. A final note to the parties, Pro Tem Judge David McDonald is next scheduled for 10/10/2019 if the parties want to continue this to his calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: MASHA LEVINSON ET AL VS. BRIAN P. MASCHLER ESQ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Comply With The Courts Order For The Production Of Documents And For Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday September 4, 2019 line 3 - DEFENDANT CARLSON, CALLADINE & PETERSON LLP's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Comply With The Courts Order For The Production Of Documents And For Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is GRANTED. Based on the declaration in support of the motion, Plaintiffs have not produced documents as ordered on April 24, 2019, or stated that all responsive documents already have been produced. It is insufficient to state that documents are in possession of the requesting party unless they are identified. CCP 2031.210(a), 2031.220 (response saying production will occur must state that all documents in demanded category in the party's possession, custody, or control, and not subject to objection, will be produced), CCP 2031.280 (documents produced must be organized to respond to RFPs or as maintained in usual course of business). Documents ordered by the court on April 24, 2019, shall be produced no later than September 12, 2019. If Plaintiffs fail to produce documents as ordered herein, Plaintiffs shall be precluded from introducing any documents covered by the order in any future proceedings, including at trial. CCP 2031.300(c). The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $3,150, payable by September 12, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S LONDON ET AL VS. CONNECT ART INTRNATIONAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiffs For In Camera Inspection Of Defendants Confidentiality Agreement And To Overrule Defendants Objection To The Deposition Of Jonathan Holland
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday September 4, 2019 line 5 - PLAINTIFFs CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S LONDON, MICHAEL SEIDENBERG and BEATRIX SEIDENBERG's Motion By Plaintiffs For In Camera Inspection Of Defendants Confidentiality Agreement And To Overrule Defendants Objection To The Deposition Of Jonathan Holland. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for in camera review is DENIED as premature. The deposition transcript indicates that Mr. Holland requested, and Plaintiffs agreed, to suspend the deposition so that Mr. Holland could seek counsel about his restrictions under the nondisparagement agreement. There is no indication that Plaintiffs have attempted to reschedule the deposition or ask questions that Mr. Holland refused to answer. The Court suggests that Plaintiffs notice a new date for continuation of the motion, and then if Mr. Holland refuses to answer questions, any party or the deponent may suspend the deposition and bring a motion under CCP 2025.420. Defendant's request for sanctions under CCP 128.5 or 128.7 is DENIED for noncompliance with CCP 125.5(f) and 128.7(c) (describing requirements for separate notice of motion and opportunity to cure). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday September 4, 2019 line 4 - DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is GRANTED. CCP 2033.220(a) requires that a party admit "so much of the matter" as is true "or as reasonably and clearly qualified by the responding party," deny the matter, or specify so much of the matter for which the party lacks sufficient information or knowledge. When a responding party "gives lack of information or knowledge as a reason for failure to admit all or part of a request for admission, that party shall state in the answer that a reasonable inquiry concerning the matter in the particular request has been made, and that the information known or readily obtainable is insufficient to enable that party to admit the matter." CCP 2033.220(b). While the authenticity of the documents referenced in RFAs 8 and 9 is contested, stating that the responding party needs a discovery response from the propounding party is not necessarily an adequate reasonable inquiry. Moreover, the objections do not raise authenticity. While the Separate Statement did not include the objections, and was therefore improper under CRC 3.1345, in the interest of resolving this dispute I exercise my discretion to consider the motion. Mills v. U.S. Bank (2008) 16 Cal.App.4th 871, 893. Based on the short response to the meet-and-confer correspondence, I find that Defendant Yan was not required to try to do more to meet-and-confer before bringing this motion. Revised responses to RFAs 8 and 9 due by September 13, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY H. KUHL VS. LANDMARK AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT LANDMARK AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Landmark American Insurance Company's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Gregory H. Kuhl's complaint is denied. The complaint's allegations support a bad faith breach of the duty to defend that render Brandt fees recoverable. Pleading the ultimate facts of intentional wrongdoing coupled with pleading of the statutory requirements for punitive damages suffices to support a claim for punitive damages. (Perkins v. Superior Court (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1, 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GREGORY H. KUHL VS. LANDMARK AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT LANDMARK AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Landmark American Insurance Company's demurrer to plaintiff Gregory H. Kuhl's complaint is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support a bad faith breach of the duty to defend by defendant by alleging that (1) defendant failed to accept a settlement offer within its policy limits that would have released all insureds; and (2) by accepting an offer that exhausted defendant's policy limits in exchange of a release of plaintiff's co-insured directors but not plaintiff. An insurer who favors the interests of one insured over those of the other violates the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. (Shell Oil Co. v. National Union Fire Ins. Co. (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1633, 1647 ["National's disbursement of its entire policy limits to indemnify S.I.P. did not discharge National's policy obligations to Shell but rather constituted an actionable breach of those duties"]; Lehto v. Allstate Ins. Co. (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 60, 72 ["An insurer owes the duty of good faith and fair dealing to each of its insureds, and cannot favor the interests of one insured over the other. [Citation.] Moreover, an insurer can breach its duty to its insureds by disbursing the policy proceeds to the insureds' claimant without first obtaining a release of the insureds."]; see also Schwartz v. State Farm Fire and Cas. Co. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 1329, 1339 ["even an insurer that pays the full limits of its policy may be liable for breach of the implied covenant, if improper claims handling causes detriment to the insured"].) Plaintiff has also alleged he has incurred economic loss proximately caused by defendant's breach: the payment of attorney's fees to the insurance coverage counsel he retained after another insurer undertook his defense under a reservation of rights. (Complaint, 9.) Unlike in Emerald Bay Community Assn. v. Golden Eagle Ins. Corp. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1078, where plaintiff was provided a defense and full coverage for the claim by its insurers and thus was "fully protected from both the expense of litigation and the exposure to liability in that lawsuit" (id. at 1094, 1096), here plaintiff alleges that not all of his expenses incurred in defending the action brought by the trustee are covered by defendant or the other insurer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
